source: Rocky Linux Admin Guide, ch. 8.3 Structure of a file system (pp. 137-141)

questions.q: Create an ext4 file system on the partition /dev/sda1.
questions.why: mkfs -t <type> makes a file system; without one, the disk space cannot be used.

questions.q: Each file system initialises a boot sector, super block, inode table and data block - with which exception?
questions.why: The swap partition is the exception to this standard structure.

questions.q: The boot sector (512 bytes) is made of three parts. What are their sizes?
options: MBR 446 bytes, DPT 64 bytes, BRID 2 bytes
MBR 64 bytes, DPT 446 bytes, BRID 2 bytes
MBR 256 bytes, DPT 254 bytes, BRID 2 bytes
questions.why: 446 (MBR/boot loader) + 64 (DPT partition table) + 2 (BRID boot record ID) = 512 bytes.

questions.q: What does the super block describe?
options: The file system itself: its name, type, status, size, free blocks, inode list, etc.
questions.why: The super block holds the metadata needed to use the FS (name, type, status, size, free blocks/inodes); a copy is kept in RAM and flushed by sync.

questions.q: Which of these is NOT stored in a file's inode?
options: The file type and access permissions
Pointers to the data blocks holding the file
questions.why: An inode holds type, permissions, owner/group, link count, size, timestamps and block pointers - but NOT the name; the name lives in the directory that references the inode.

questions.q: What does the size of the inode table determine?
options: The maximum number of files the file system can contain
The maximum size of any single file
questions.why: Each file needs an inode, so the inode table's size caps the number of files the FS can hold.

questions.q: Check and repair the file system on /dev/sda1.
questions.why: fsck checks a file system's consistency and offers to repair inconsistencies.

questions.q: What must be true of a partition before you run fsck on it?
options: It must be an LVM volume
It must contain a swap area
questions.why: The partition to be checked must be unmounted (for the root FS, use a /forcefsck file or shutdown -F to check at boot).

questions.q: After an fsck repair, where are files that no longer have an inode-table entry placed?
questions.why: Recovered but unreferenced files are attached to the logical drive's /lost+found folder.
